definition: One beyond training, classically the arahant who has completed the path.
notes: The project prefers beyond training because it keeps the doctrinal contrast with sekha explicit. Fully trained is acceptable in explanatory prose when the sentence wants a more idiomatic English phrase. In practice the term points to completed training, so it belongs closely with arahant and release language rather than with general maturity or expertise.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a smoother English phrase is needed
When the passage stresses completion of the path rather than ongoing cultivation
context_rules.notes: Preserve the contrast with the trainee rather than softening it into a generic compliment.

example_phrases.translation: conduct, composure, and discernment beyond training
example_phrases.notes: Useful for the completion side of the training contrast.
